description:
You are maintaining a tiny utility project located in /home/user/utils. Inside that directory the following two files already exist: 1. /home/user/utils/VERSION – currently contains a single line with the text 1.4.2 2. /home/user/utils/CHANGELOG.md – currently contains: 
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [1.4.2] - 2023-09-28
### Added
- Initial release of data-parsing utilities.
Your task is to perform a patch-level semantic version bump and record the change in the changelog. Target end-state that the automated tests will check: A. VERSION bump • The file /home/user/utils/VERSION must end with a single newline and contain exactly 1.4.3 B. Changelog entry • A new section has to be inserted at the **top** of CHANGELOG.md (directly below the introductory paragraph and above the existing ## [1.4.2] section) with **exactly** the following seven lines, including blank lines and markdown punctuation: 
## [1.4.3] - 2023-10-05
### Fixed
- Handle '--help' flag gracefully when no subcommand is provided.
• After your change, the complete file must be (blank lines are significant): 
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [1.4.3] - 2023-10-05
### Fixed
- Handle '--help' flag gracefully when no subcommand is provided.

## [1.4.2] - 2023-09-28
### Added
- Initial release of data-parsing utilities.
C. Bump log • Create a new file /home/user/utils/bump.log. • It must contain exactly one line, no extra whitespace: 1.4.2->1.4.3 Use any short sequence of shell commands you like (roughly 2-3 commands is enough) to achieve this. After running the commands you should be able to: 1. cat /home/user/utils/VERSION → prints “1.4.3” 2. head -n 8 /home/user/utils/CHANGELOG.md → shows the new entry as specified 3. cat /home/user/utils/bump.log → prints “1.4.2->1.4.3” Do **not** modify any other files or directories. The automated test will verify the three files exactly as described above (content, order, newlines).
